input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:

and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content: `.testsettings` files are **not supported** in modern MSTest (v3+). You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's the equivalent for your configuration:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / TestDirectory useLoadContext -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Replaces TestTimeout property (30 000 ms) -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the CodeCoverage data collector from .testsettings -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file with:
1. Assembly resolution config
2. TestTimeout of 30000ms (30 seconds)
3. Code coverage data collector

With MSTest v3+, `.testsettings` files are no longer supported. They need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file.

Let me create a `.runsettings` file that replaces their `.testsettings` configuration.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / TestDirectory useLoadContext -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Replaces TestTimeout property (30 000 ms) -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Replaces the CodeCoverage data collector from .testsettings -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
Here's a summary of the migration:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `TestDirectory useLoadContext` | `<DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>` |
| `TestTimeout` (30 000 ms) | `<TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>` |
| CodeCoverage DataCollector | Same concept, updated URI (`/2.0`) |

**To use it**, either:
- Run: `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`
- Or add `<RunSettingsFilePath>local.runsettings</RunSettingsFilePath>` to a `<PropertyGroup>` in your `.csproj`

You can safely delete `local.testsettings` — it's no longer read by the test runner.
